---
title: "Installation & Setup"
---

## Installing because-py

because-py is the Python NumPyro backend for the R [`because`](https://because-pkg.github.io) package.
It can be used from R (the recommended approach) or directly in Python.

---

## From R (recommended)

### Step 1 — Install the `because` R package

```r
# Stable release
remotes::install_github("because-pkg/because@v1.3.0", build_vignettes = TRUE)

# Latest development version
remotes::install_github("because-pkg/because", build_vignettes = TRUE)
```

### Step 2 — Install reticulate

```r
install.packages("reticulate")
```

### Step 3 — Install Python (if needed)

If you do not already have Python on your system, `reticulate` can install
Miniconda for you (skip this if Python is already installed):

```r
reticulate::install_miniconda()
```

### Step 4 — One-step setup

`because` provides a built-in helper that installs all required Python packages
(`numpyro`, `jax`, `jaxlib`, `networkx`, `funsor`, and `because_py`) into your
active Python environment:

```r
install_because_numpyro()
```

The function automatically detects your active Python environment — including
RStudio project-level virtual environments (`.venv`) and any existing `reticulate`
configuration — falling back to `"r-reticulate"` if none is found.

### Troubleshooting: wrong Python environment

If in a fresh R session `reticulate` picks up a different Python environment than
the one where the packages were installed, `because` will throw:

```
Error: Failed to import python module 'because.api'.
Python is currently running from: /path/to/wrong/python
Ensure because_py is installed in this exact environment.
Try running: install_because_numpyro()
```

In this case, point `reticulate` to the correct environment before calling `because()`:

```r
reticulate::use_virtualenv("r-reticulate", required = TRUE)
```

---

## Directly in Python

```bash
pip install git+https://github.com/because-pkg/because_py.git
```

Or from source:

```bash
git clone https://github.com/because-pkg/because_py.git
cd because_py
pip install -e .
```
